verses.text: Hebrew servants serve six years and go free in the seventh, with what they brought. Servant law. Freedom is the rule, not perpetual bondage.
Hebrew servants serve six years and go free in the seventh, with what they brought. Servant law. Freedom is the rule, not perpetual bondage.
Hebrew servants serve six years and go free in the seventh, with what they brought. Servant law. Freedom is the rule, not perpetual bondage.
Hebrew servants serve six years and go free in the seventh, with what they brought. Servant law. Freedom is the rule, not perpetual bondage.
One who loves his master and will not go free is bound to him for ever. Willing service. Love may choose to stay and serve.
One who loves his master and will not go free is bound to him for ever. Willing service. Love may choose to stay and serve.
A maidservant has her own protections; if mistreated, she goes free without money. Maid's rights. God guards the vulnerable in bondage.
A maidservant has her own protections; if mistreated, she goes free without money. Maid's rights. God guards the vulnerable in bondage.
A maidservant has her own protections; if mistreated, she goes free without money. Maid's rights. God guards the vulnerable in bondage.

A maidservant has her own protections; if mistreated, she goes free without money. Maid's rights. God guards the vulnerable in bondage.
He that smites a man to death shall die, but the unintentional may flee to a place God appoints. Murder vs. mischance. God weighs intent.
He that smites a man to death shall die, but the unintentional may flee to a place God appoints. Murder vs. mischance. God weighs intent.
He that smites a man to death shall die, but the unintentional may flee to a place God appoints. Murder vs. mischance. God weighs intent.
He that smites or curses father or mother shall die; so shall the manstealer. Honour. Crimes against family and freedom are capital.
He that smites or curses father or mother shall die; so shall the manstealer. Honour. Crimes against family and freedom are capital.
He that smites or curses father or mother shall die; so shall the manstealer. Honour. Crimes against family and freedom are capital.
If a man wounds another in a quarrel, he pays for the lost time and the healing. Restitution. Harm done must be made good.
If a man wounds another in a quarrel, he pays for the lost time and the healing. Restitution. Harm done must be made good.
A master who kills his servant under his hand is to be punished. Accountable. Even a slave's life is protected.
A master who kills his servant under his hand is to be punished. Accountable. Even a slave's life is protected.
For hurt to a woman with child, 'life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth.' Just measure. The penalty is fitted to the harm.
For hurt to a woman with child, 'life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth.' Just measure. The penalty is fitted to the harm.
For hurt to a woman with child, 'life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth.' Just measure. The penalty is fitted to the harm.
For hurt to a woman with child, 'life for life, eye for eye, tooth for tooth.' Just measure. The penalty is fitted to the harm.
A servant maimed by his master goes free for the injury. Mercy. Cruelty forfeits the master's right.
A servant maimed by his master goes free for the injury. Mercy. Cruelty forfeits the master's right.
A goring ox is stoned; a careless owner who was warned is liable. Negligence. Owners answer for known dangers.
A goring ox is stoned; a careless owner who was warned is liable. Negligence. Owners answer for known dangers.

He that opens a pit, or whose ox harms another's, must make it good. Make good. Carelessness must repay the loss.
